text: The biological plausibility linking increases in oxidative stress to reactive oxygen species (ROS) is strong.  Reactive oxygen species (ROS) are produced by many normal cellular processes (ex. cellular respiration, mitochondrial electron transport, specialized enzyme reactions) and occur in multiple chemical forms (ex. superoxide anion, hydroxyl radical, hydrogen peroxide).  Antioxidant enzymes play a major role in reducing reactive oxygen species (ROS) levels in cells (Ray et al. 2012) to prevent cellular damage to lipids, proteins, and DNA (Juan et al. 2021).  Oxidative stress occurs when antioxidant enzymes do not prevent ROS levels from increasing in cells, often induced by environmental stressors (biological, chemical, radiation).
